name: Notion
description: Send authenticated requests to the Notion API for pages, data sources, blocks, comments, search, and user resources.

actions.name: Notion API Request
actions.description: Call any Notion API endpoint with a raw HTTP configuration.

connectionId.description: Connection id for the authorized Notion workspace

method.description: HTTP method for the Notion API call

Notion-Version.description: Notion API version header. Use `2026-03-11` for current production usage.

headers.description: HTTP headers for the Notion API request. Authorization is injected automatically from the connection.

body.description: Optional JSON body for POST and PATCH requests

outputSchema.description: Raw JSON response body returned by the Notion API

actions.name: Search
actions.description: Search pages and data sources in a Notion workspace.

connectionId.description: Connection id for the authorized Notion workspace

Notion-Version.description: Notion API version header. Use `2026-03-11` for current production usage.

headers.description: HTTP headers for the Notion API request. Authorization is injected automatically from the connection.

body.description: Notion search request body. Example: { query, filter, sort, start_cursor, page_size }

filter.description: The filter to apply. Example: { property: "object", value: "page" } or { property: "object", value: "data_source" }.

sort.description: The sort order to apply. Example: { direction: "ascending", timestamp: "last_edited_time" }.

start_cursor.description: If supplied, this endpoint will return a page of results starting after the cursor provided. If not supplied, the API will return the first page of results.

page_size.description: The number of items from the full list to return in a single page. Maximum: 100.

outputSchema.description: Raw JSON response body returned by the Notion API

actions.name: Create Page
actions.description: Create a new Notion page in a parent page or database.

connectionId.description: Connection id for the authorized Notion workspace

Notion-Version.description: Notion API version header. Use `2026-03-11` for current production usage.

headers.description: HTTP headers for the Notion API request. Authorization is injected automatically from the connection.

body.description: Create page payload. Must include parent and properties, and may include children, icon, or cover.

parent.description: A parent object representing where the page is created. Must be a `page_id` or `database_id`. Example: `{ "page_id": "YOUR_PAGE_ID" }` or `{ "database_id": "YOUR_DATABASE_ID" }`.

page_id.description: The ID of the parent page.

database_id.description: The ID of the parent database.

properties.description: Property values for the page. If creating a page within a database, these must conform to the database's property schema. For a top-level page, a 'title' property is generally required. Example: `{ "Name": { "title": [ { "text": { "content": "New Page Title" } } ] } }`.

children.description: An array of block objects to add as children to the page. Example: `[ { "object": "block", "type": "paragraph", "paragraph": { "rich_text": [ { "type": "text", "text": { "content": "Hello World!" } } ] } } ]`.

icon.description: Page icon object (emoji, external, or file). Example: `{ "type": "emoji", "emoji": "👋" }`.

cover.description: Page cover object (external or file). Example: `{ "type": "external", "external": { "url": "https://example.com/cover.jpg" } }`.

outputSchema.description: Raw JSON response body returned by the Notion API

connectionId.description: Connection id for the authorized Notion workspace

url.description: Full Notion API URL for the page, including its ID. Example: `https://api.notion.com/v1/pages/f7e4d8c6-a2b1-4f0e-8c9d-1e0f2b3a4c5d`

Notion-Version.description: Notion API version header. Use `2026-03-11` for current production usage.

headers.description: HTTP headers for the Notion API request. Authorization is injected automatically from the connection.

queryParams.description: Optional query parameters to filter returned properties.

filter_properties.description: List of page property IDs to fetch and return in the response. If not specified, all properties are returned. Example: `["title_id", "status_id"]`

outputSchema.description: Raw JSON response body returned by the Notion API

actions.name: Update Page
actions.description: Update page properties, icon, cover, or archive state.

connectionId.description: Connection id for the authorized Notion workspace

url.description: Full Notion API URL for the page to update, including its ID. Example: `https://api.notion.com/v1/pages/f7e4d8c6-a2b1-4f0e-8c9d-1e0f2b3a4c5d`

Notion-Version.description: Notion API version header. Use `2026-03-11` for current production usage.

headers.description: HTTP headers for the Notion API request. Authorization is injected automatically from the connection.

body.description: Update page payload. Example fields: properties, archived, in_trash, icon, cover.

properties.description: Property values to update for the page. Example: `{ "Status": { "status": { "name": "Done" } } }`.

icon.description: New page icon object (emoji, external, or file). To remove an icon, set to null. Example: `{ "type": "emoji", "emoji": "🎉" }`.

cover.description: New page cover object (external or file). To remove a cover, set to null. Example: `{ "type": "external", "external": { "url": "https://example.com/new_cover.jpg" } }`.

archived.description: Whether the page is archived. Setting this to `true` will move the page to the trash. `false` will unarchive it. Defaults to `false`.

in_trash.description: Whether the page is in the trash. Setting this to `true` will move the page to the trash. `false` will restore it.

outputSchema.description: Raw JSON response body returned by the Notion API

actions.name: Query Data Source
actions.description: Query entries from a Notion data source (database). Uses the current /v1/data_sources endpoint.

connectionId.description: Connection id for the authorized Notion workspace

url.description: Full Notion API URL for querying the data source, including its ID. Example: `https://api.notion.com/v1/data_sources/f7e4d8c6-a2b1-4f0e-8c9d-1e0f2b3a4c5d/query`

Notion-Version.description: Notion API version header. Use `2026-03-11` for current production usage.

headers.description: HTTP headers for the Notion API request. Authorization is injected automatically from the connection.

filter_properties.description: List of property IDs or names to include in the response. Reduces response size and improves performance. Example: `["title", "status"]`
filter_properties.x-label: Filter Properties (Array of IDs or names)

body.description: Query payload. Example fields: filter, sorts, start_cursor, page_size, in_trash, result_type.

filter.description: A filter object that limits the results. Example: `{ "property": "Status", "status": { "equals": "Done" } }`.

sorts.description: An array of sort objects that orders the results. Example: `[ { "property": "Created", "direction": "descending" } ]`.

start_cursor.description: If supplied, this endpoint will return a page of results starting after the cursor provided. If not supplied, the API will return the first page of results.

page_size.description: The number of items from the full list to return in a single page. Maximum: 100.

in_trash.description: If true, only returns entries that are in the trash. Defaults to false.

result_type.description: Optionally filter results to only pages or only data sources. Useful for wiki databases. Defaults to returning both.

outputSchema.description: Raw JSON response body returned by the Notion API

connectionId.description: Connection id for the authorized Notion workspace

url.description: Full Notion API URL for retrieving block children, including the parent block's ID. Example: `https://api.notion.com/v1/blocks/f7e4d8c6-a2b1-4f0e-8c9d-1e0f2b3a4c5d/children`

Notion-Version.description: Notion API version header. Use `2026-03-11` for current production usage.

headers.description: HTTP headers for the Notion API request. Authorization is injected automatically from the connection.

queryParams.description: Optional query parameters such as start_cursor or page_size

start_cursor.description: If supplied, this endpoint will return a page of results starting after the cursor provided.

page_size.description: The number of items from the full list to return in a single page. Maximum: 100.

outputSchema.description: Raw JSON response body returned by the Notion API

actions.name: Append Block Children
actions.description: Append child blocks to an existing block.

connectionId.description: Connection id for the authorized Notion workspace

url.description: Full Notion API URL for appending block children, including the parent block's ID. Example: `https://api.notion.com/v1/blocks/f7e4d8c6-a2b1-4f0e-8c9d-1e0f2b3a4c5d/children`

Notion-Version.description: Notion API version header. Use `2026-03-11` for current production usage.

headers.description: HTTP headers for the Notion API request. Authorization is injected automatically from the connection.

body.description: Append children payload. Includes a children array and optionally after.

children.description: An array of block objects to add as children. Example: `[ { "object": "block", "type": "paragraph", "paragraph": { "rich_text": [ { "type": "text", "text": { "content": "New paragraph content." } } ] } } ]`.

after.description: The ID of the existing block directly after which the new blocks should be inserted. If not provided, new blocks are appended to the end.

outputSchema.description: Raw JSON response body returned by the Notion API

actions.name: Create Comment
actions.description: Create a comment on a page or block discussion thread.

connectionId.description: Connection id for the authorized Notion workspace

Notion-Version.description: Notion API version header. Use `2026-03-11` for current production usage.

headers.description: HTTP headers for the Notion API request. Authorization is injected automatically from the connection.

body.description: Create comment payload. Must follow Notion's comments API shape.

parent.description: The parent of the comment. Must be a `page_id` or `block_id`. Example: `{ "page_id": "YOUR_PAGE_ID" }` or `{ "block_id": "YOUR_BLOCK_ID" }`.

page_id.description: The ID of the parent page.

block_id.description: The ID of the parent block.

discussion_id.description: If this comment is a reply to an existing discussion thread, provide the ID of that discussion.

rich_text.description: An array of rich text objects for the comment content. Example: `[ { "type": "text", "text": { "content": "This is a new comment." } } ]`.

outputSchema.description: Raw JSON response body returned by the Notion API

actions.name: List Users
actions.description: List users and bots in the connected Notion workspace.

connectionId.description: Connection id for the authorized Notion workspace

Notion-Version.description: Notion API version header. Use `2026-03-11` for current production usage.

headers.description: HTTP headers for the Notion API request. Authorization is injected automatically from the connection.

queryParams.description: Optional query parameters such as start_cursor or page_size

start_cursor.description: If supplied, this endpoint will return a page of results starting after the cursor provided.

page_size.description: The number of items from the full list to return in a single page. Maximum: 100.

outputSchema.description: Raw JSON response body returned by the Notion API
